No, Steve Sekely and William King Baggot were not known by multiple names or nicknames. 

Steve Sekely was a Hungarian film director, known for directing several films, including *Lady in the Death House*, *Flying Gold*, and *Number 111*. He was born as Székely István and used several names due to his changing professional and immigration status.

William King Baggot was an American actor, film director, and screenwriter. He was known as "The Most Photographed Man in the World" and "King of the Movies." He was born as William King Baggot and was not known by multiple names or nicknames. 

Thus, they were not known by multiple names or nicknames.
